abstract: We report the development of an iron-catalyzed strategy for the intermolecular 1,2-addition of perfluoroalkyl iodides to alkenes and alkynes. This catalytic system exhibits broad substrate compatibility and excellent functional-group tolerance, facilitating the incorporation of diverse perfluoroalkyl groups, including trifluoromethyl (CF₃). The resulting perfluoroalkylated alkenyl and alkyl iodides serve as versatile synthetic intermediates that can be readily elaborated through subsequent cross-coupling transformations. Overall, this methodology offers a robust and efficient platform for the streamlined synthesis of complex perfluoroalkylated organic molecules.
